note: iter-2 = first actual Phase-3 pass (Phase 3 deferred from iter-1 by a mid-run branch update). Full Phase-3 roster run on the final committed HEAD.
phase2_note: Mechanical coverage-map/registry/run.sh contracts confirmed by the green full suite (9594 passed, 0 failed) per operator directive; verifiers not re-dispatched.

phase3_findings.description: capability-profiles.sh T8 no-runtime-read invariant iterates a hardcoded 6-workflow list with no lower bound; a renamed/deleted/new workflow escapes the guard (fails open).
coverage_map_guard.py _git_tracked uses text=True; a non-ASCII tracked path would raise UnicodeDecodeError (outside the except tuple) — still fails closed, breadcrumb-quality gap only.
DEVFLOW_SYSTEM_OVERVIEW 'a new depth-1 unit ... at any depth' phrasing fights itself (clarity, TRUE not false).
coverage-map.json generated_by scanner sits in slight tension with 'hand-maintained / scanning is a non-goal' prose (clarity, not false).
DEVFLOW_SYSTEM_OVERVIEW 'a ranked to-do list' overstates — the map carries no ranking field (clarity).
main() clean-tree rc-0 path only covered transitively via run.sh, not a unit assertion.
test_green_shipped_tree splits git ls-files on generic whitespace (guard uses split('\n')).
evaluate() violation result is stringly-typed ([armN] prefix); a Violation NamedTuple would decouple tests from breadcrumb text.
files keys and non_code_exempt not required disjoint; a path in both is representable and uncaught.
cross-constant invariant among PATTERNS/CODE_EXTS/TOP_DIRS unenforced; a module-load assert would pin it.
note field on files/run_sh_blocks entries is neither validated nor used (trivial).

completeness_critic.independent_signal: raw git ls-files depth-1 code exts
completeness_critic.result: audit matched set is a superset of the independent enumeration (guard rc 0 on shipped tree; config_fingerprint.py + preflight.py both present) — complete w.r.t. the independent signal

dirty_tree: clean — no agent mutated the tree during Phase 3 dispatch

fix_decisions.evidence: Pre-existing behavior extracted verbatim from run.sh #561 block; not introduced by this PR's logic. The no-runtime-read invariant is backstopped by cloud_writer_contract + install-loop pins. Revisit condition: a 7th workflow reads the manifest at runtime.

All remaining findings are Suggestion-severity clarity nits (TRUE not false per comment-analyzer), minor test_gaps transitively covered by the green suite, or type-design polish explicitly graded nice-to-have. Below the operator's Critical/Important merge gate. Parked as advisory per receiving-code-review; not worth a fix-loop iteration on an otherwise-clean engine-self-modifying PR.

shadow.reason: Per operator efficiency directive: the full 6-agent Phase-3 fan-out + completeness critic already ran clean (0 Critical/0 Important) on the final HEAD and the final-pass reviewer independently returned Ready-to-merge; the green suite mechanically covers the contracts. A redundant full shadow re-run was not performed. Formal terminal verdict therefore carries a shadow-coverage caveat; the operator's separate /devflow:review is the exhaustiveness check if desired.

verification_evidence.suite: 9594 passed, 0 failed, 1 skipped (dirty-tree self-skip of #434, runs in CI)
